members.name: Index
members.summary: The drag event index in the case of a multi-drag event.

members.name: PenInverted
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] when using the eraser end of a stylus pen.

members.name: Position
members.summary: The drag position in the viewport the node is in, using the coordinate system of this viewport.

members.name: Pressure
members.summary: Represents the pressure the user puts on the pen. Ranges from [code]0.0[/code] to [code]1.0[/code].

members.name: Relative
members.summary: The drag position relative to the previous position (position at the last frame). [b]Note:[/b] [member relative] is automatically scaled according to the content scale factor, which is defined by the project's stretch mode settings. This means touch sensitivity will appear different depending on resolution when using [member relative] in a script that handles touch aiming. To avoid this, use [member screen_relative] instead.

members.name: ScreenRelative
members.summary: The unscaled drag position relative to the previous position in screen coordinates (position at the last frame). This position is [i]not[/i] scaled according to the content scale factor or calls to [method InputEvent.xformed_by]. This should be preferred over [member relative] for touch aiming regardless of the project's stretch mode.

members.name: ScreenVelocity
members.summary: The unscaled drag velocity in pixels per second in screen coordinates. This velocity is [i]not[/i] scaled according to the content scale factor or calls to [method InputEvent.xformed_by]. This should be preferred over [member velocity] for touch aiming regardless of the project's stretch mode.

members.name: Tilt
members.summary: Represents the angles of tilt of the pen. Positive X-coordinate value indicates a tilt to the right. Positive Y-coordinate value indicates a tilt toward the user. Ranges from [code]-1.0[/code] to [code]1.0[/code] for both axes.

members.name: Velocity
members.summary: The drag velocity. [b]Note:[/b] [member velocity] is automatically scaled according to the content scale factor, which is defined by the project's stretch mode settings. This means touch sensitivity will appear different depending on resolution when using [member velocity] in a script that handles touch aiming. To avoid this, use [member screen_velocity] instead.
